text: The robotic fourth arm grasps the cornea, incorporating the round ligament at a perpendicular angle, and applies cranial superior traction to expose the triangle of freedom in the retroperitoneal space.
The robotic arm grasps the ovarian vessels, applying medial traction to facilitate entry into the retroperitoneum, similar to the laparoscopic approach.
The surgeon extends the incision parallel to the ovarian vessels using a spread and lift technique, visibly manipulating the tissue to widen the surgical field.
The surgeon meticulously dissects inferiorly through the identified areolar tissue until the posterior peritoneum becomes visible.
The surgeon uses robotic assistance to follow the dissection plan inferiorly towards the ureter, maintaining the exposure of the surgical field.
The surgeon uses a grasper to apply counter traction while employing a push and spread technique to carefully advance along the ureter, ensuring precise manipulation within the surgical field.
The surgeon identifies the ureter and safely dissects the superior areolar tissue to clear the path along the ureter.
The surgeon uses a gentle probe and spread technique to carefully manipulate the tissue, avoiding trauma to the uterine vessels.
